summary: Closed-unmerged tooling PR that tried to add generated Ninja targets for formatting changed C/C++ lines. It added `tools/format.py` as a Python wrapper around `git clang-format`, emitted `format` and `format-check` targets from `tools/project.py`, and updated contributing docs to recommend `ninja format` / `ninja format-check`. The main review objection was that this did not satisfy the likely intent of issue #1543: Ninja should format individual files so it can track dirty inputs efficiently; wrapping the whole `git-clang-format` operation in one target provides little utility compared with the existing pre-commit hook setup.
what_changed: Added new `tools/format.py` script with an argparse CLI for `base`, `--check`, and `--clang-format`.
The script finds the git worktree root with `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`, checks for `clang-format` and `git-clang-format`, then runs `git clang-format --binary <clang-format>`.
`--check` mode adds `--diff` and returns failure when the produced stdout contains `diff --git`, so formatting differences fail the Ninja target without modifying files.

Updated `tools/project.py` to emit Ninja rules `clang_format` and `clang_format_check`, and build targets `format` and `format-check` with implicit dependencies on `tools/format.py` and `.clang-format`.
Updated `.github/CONTRIBUTING.md` to replace direct `git clang-format` instructions with `ninja format` and `ninja format-check` after project configuration.

Author validation included `python3 -m py_compile tools/format.py tools/project.py configure.py`, `python3 configure.py`, `git diff --check`, direct script runs, `ninja -t targets`, `ninja format-check`, and `ninja format`.
smart_moves: Kept formatting scoped to changed C/C++ lines by reusing `git clang-format` instead of reformatting the whole repository.
Added a non-mutating `format-check` path suitable for CI or local validation.
Checked missing `clang-format`, missing `git-clang-format`, and outside-worktree failure cases to print direct errors instead of Python tracebacks.

Made `.clang-format` and the formatting script implicit dependencies of the Ninja targets, so generated build files at least know those target definitions depend on formatting config and wrapper code.
decomp_lessons: Formatting tooling in a decomp repository should avoid large unrelated whitespace churn; changed-line formatting is desirable for reviewability.

If the goal is a Ninja formatting target, wrapping one global `git-clang-format` command may not be enough: Ninja's strength is tracking file-level dirty inputs and running per-file commands incrementally.

Before adding new build targets, verify the target matches the motivating issue's workflow model; here the reviewer said issue #1543 was about per-file `clang-format` tracking, not just exposing `git-clang-format` through Ninja.
When an existing pre-commit hook already covers the workflow, a docs/dependency update may be a better fit than adding new build-system machinery.
naming_conventions: Public Ninja targets used hyphenated names: `format` and `format-check`.
Internal Ninja rule names used snake_case: `clang_format` and `clang_format_check`.
The wrapper script was named generically as `tools/format.py` and described its operation as formatting changed C/C++ lines using `git clang-format`.
CLI option names followed conventional long-form flags: `--check` and `--clang-format`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly-specific matching tactic was introduced.
Indirect matching hygiene lesson: use changed-line formatting to keep decomp diffs focused and avoid formatting churn obscuring code-matching work.
review_feedback: ribbanya said they did not see the utility of this implementation.
Reviewer interpretation of #1543: the desired implementation was to run each file through `clang-format` individually so Ninja can track dirty files and format efficiently.
Reviewer argued that outsourcing the entire format operation to `git-clang-format` through Python was less compelling than a commit hook.
Reviewer noted a pre-commit setup already exists in `.pre-commit-config.yaml` and suggested documenting pre-commit installation and possibly adding it to `requirements.in`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that resolved or revisited issue #1543.
Search for later changes adding `format` or `format-check` targets to `tools/project.py`.
Search for PRs documenting pre-commit installation or adding pre-commit to `requirements.in`.
Check whether `.pre-commit-config.yaml` became the preferred clang-format workflow after this PR closed.
